title: Dark Roasted Japanese Pork Ramen
description: A midnight-craving Japanese dish featuring shredded roasted pork, lacquer-glaze, and ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly ingredients, this dark roasted Japanese pork ramen is a hearty and satisfying meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Whisk together soy sauce, sake, mirin, and brown sugar in a small bowl.
Place the pork shoulder in a large Dutch oven and brush the soy sauce mixture evenly over the pork.
Roast the pork in the preheated oven for 2 hours, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 190 degrees Fahrenheit.
Remove the pork from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before shredding it into bite-sized pieces.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the ramen noodles according to the package instructions.
Drain the noodles and divide them among four bowls.
Top each bowl with shredded pork, green onions, and sesame seeds.
Drizzle the lacquer-glaze sauce over the top of each bowl and serve immediately.
